entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Iran plans to send Russia drones to replenish weapons lost in invasion
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe at slain former leader’s funeral
  • Within the war between Russia and Ukraine, a war between Chechens
  • Uber Files put Macron’s neoliberalism in the spotlight
  • Iran to send hundreds of drones to Russia for use in Ukraine, U.S. says
  • White House says Iran may provide Russia with hundreds of drones
  • Russian strikes kill at least three in Kharkiv and injure dozens
  • Who could replace Boris Johnson as British prime minister?
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Macron’s Uber dealings may prompt parliamentary inquiry in France
  • World population to reach 8 billion by November even as growth slows
  • Uber wooed Russia’s rich and powerful but failed there anyway
  • As Uber steamrolled into France, Emmanuel Macron was a ‘true ally’
  • Europe braces for gas ‘nightmare’ as pipeline from Russia shuts off
  • Wildfires rage in Portugal as punishing heat wave sweeps Europe
  • The scene after a Russian missile strike on residences in the eastern Ukrainian city of Chasiv Yar
  • Sri Lankan president confirms move to quit as protesters occupy his home
  • Protesting bank depositors in China beaten by mob as police stand by
  • Shinzo Abe’s family holds private wake for slain former Japanese leader
  • Last year, Cubans took to the streets. Now they’re fleeing the island.
  • Some Ukrainians won't flee areas caught in crosshairs of war
  • UN projects world population will reach 8 billion on Nov. 15
  • On protest anniversary, Cuba, US far apart on what happened
  • Russia wins fight to limit aid to Syrian rebels to 6 months
  • UN envoy to seek longer, expanded truce in Yemen civil war
  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Pa. town broke law in recent hiring of Tamir Rice’s killer, A.G. says
  • Gerald McEntee, longtime president of AFSCME labor union, dies at 87
  • His McDonald’s closed for months due to renovations. He kept paying the staff.
  • Kyrgios wore a red cap to Wimbledon. Here’s why that’s controversial.
  • Buttigieg says officials like Kavanaugh ‘should expect’ public protest
  • Post Politics Now: ‘It matters, but it’s not enough,’ Biden says of new gun law
  • Jan. 6 hearing expected to focus on link between militants, White House
  • Parkland dad disrupts White House event on gun control
  • Judge rejects Bannon’s bid to delay trial, executive-privilege claim
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • About the Uber Files investigation
  • Uber promised South Africans better lives but knew drivers risked debt and danger
  • Twitter shares plunge as it braces for messy legal battle with Elon Musk
  • Uber leveraged violent attacks against its drivers to pressure politicians
  • In leaked memo, Facebook tells managers low performers don’t belong
  • You can now preview iOS 16’s editable iMessages and more
  • As Musk moves to abandon deal, Twitter faces ‘worst case scenario’
  • What’s behind rising rents and what can be done?
  • Homeowner recommends whole house water shut-off valves
  • 3 bedroom townhouse-style condo in Germantown lists below $340,000
  • Britain’s Imperial Nostalgia Should Follow Johnson Out
  • Benettons Look Past Travel Chaos in Dufry Deal
  • The Uber Files Show Just How Far Macron Has Come
  • Unicorn Stock Markets: A Lifeboat for Startup Employees?
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why US Sees Saudi Arabia Again as Partner, Not Pariah
  • Why Central Banks Got Serious About Digital Money
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Political and Economic Crisis and What It Means
  • Biden’s trip to Saudi Arabia erodes our moral authority
  • How Cuba’s investment in writers and artists came back to haunt its regime
  • The U.S. should support Japan’s move to legitimize its military
  • Is there a method behind Musk’s Twitter-deal weirdness?
  • If Herschel Walker wins in Georgia, America will have lost its mind
  • Shorten the war. Send 60 HIMARS to Ukraine.
  • As Latino voters shift right, here’s where Democrats will be hit hardest
  • We should worry what happens if Trump never faces justice, not if he does
  • Democrats are overreacting about the Supreme Court
  • Now Bannon wants to squawk
  • Biden needs to address the coming crisis between Israel and Palestine
  • Middle East policy is too important to be guided by short-term concerns
  • Confirm Hanes to the Eastern District
  • Three woofs for Michael Gerson
  • More potential names for military bases
  • Reduce the stigma to help people with substance use disorder
  • A heartbreaking decision on guns
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Relief Eludes Many Renters as Fed Raises Interest Rates
  • Webb Space Telescope: Biden and NASA Reveal First Image
  • Greg Robinson Fixed NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope, Reluctantly
  • Hutchinson Testimony Jolts Justice Dept. to Discuss Trump’s Conduct More Openly
  • Biden’s Approval Hits 33 Percent; Democrats Want 2024 Options, Poll Shows
  • Russia Steps Up Attacks on Civilian Areas, Even With Advance Paused
  • How Elon Musk Damaged Twitter and Left It Worse Off
  • What Is Delaware’s Court of Chancery and Its Role in Elon Musk’s Twitter Deal?
  • A Glimpse Inside a Florentine Silk-Weaving Workshop
  • Joe Biden Is Too Old to Be President Again
  • The Rajapaksa Family Promised Sri Lanka Prosperity, but Brought Destitution
  • Male Birth Control Is Needed Post-Roe v. Wade
  • Merrick Garland Should Investigate Trump’s 2020 Election Schemes as a ‘Hub and Spoke’ Conspiracy
  • Even if Republicans Outlaw Abortion, Americans Will Soon Rebel
  • And the Next President Will Be …
  • ‘Stay and Fight’ Isn’t So Easy When a Red State Puts Your Child at Risk
  • Olympian Mo Farah Says He Was Trafficked to the UK as a Child
  • Rivals Vying to Replace Johnson Are Diverse in Background, Not in Plans
  • The Google One-Star Review Scam Targeting Restaurants
  • 40 Years Later, Nevada Man Is Charged With Murdering a 5-Year-Old Girl
  • The Power of a Casual Check-in Text
  • Ukraine Live Updates: U.S. Says Russia Is Looking to Iran for Drones
  • Ukrainian Medic’s Months in Russian Cell: Cold, Dirty and Used as a Prop
  • Where Mantras Trump Medicine, Vaccines Are a ‘Violation’
  • ‘There’s Nothing’: Scarcity Deepens Desperation in Sri Lanka
  • Cambridge Opens an Inquiry After Student Deaths
  • Security Forces in China Attack Protesters Seeking Frozen Funds
  • Russia Repeatedly Strikes Ukraine’s Civilians. There’s Always an Excuse.
  • Suspect in Abe Shooting Held a ‘Grudge.’ Scrutiny Falls on a Church.
  • A Year After Mass Protest, Cubans Face Stark Choice: ‘Prison or Exile’
  • Three Cities in China Impose Partial Lockdowns and Macau Shuts Casinos
  • Your Tuesday Briefing
  • Russia Is Getting Iran to Send Hundreds of Drones, White House Says
  • Your Tuesday Briefing: Bank Protests in China
  • Putin Extends Fast-Track Russian Citizenship Process to All Ukrainians
  • E.U. Health Agency Recommends Second Covid Booster for Older People
  • Dueling Weaknesses
  • Brittney Griner Is Honored by Fellow Players at W.N.B.A. All-Star Game
  • A survivor emerges from the rubble of an apartment complex.
  • What We Know About the Protests in Sri Lanka
  • In Eastern Ukraine, Attacks Intensify as Russia Readies New Offensive
  • What We Know About the Shinzo Abe Assassination
  • States With Abortion Bans Risk Losing Their Economic Edge
  • For Uvalde Families, a Call for Answers Grows Louder
  • Trump Loses Support of Half of GOP Voters, Poll Finds
  • Biden Administration May Offer Second Coronavirus Boosters to All Adults
  • Crime Wave at 7-Eleven Stores in California Leaves 2 Dead, Police Say
  • Raskin Brings Expertise on Right-Wing Extremism to Jan. 6 Inquiry
  • Justice Department Taps Oregon Official to Run Troubled Bureau of Prisons
  • An Anti-Trump Republican Group Is Back for the Midterms
  • Gerald W. McEntee, Union Leader With Political Clout, Dies at 87
  • Biden Calls for More Gun Control at White House Event
  • Senate Absences Snarl Democrats’ Plans for a Quick Return to Business
  • Albuquerque Police Investigate Teenager’s Death in Standoff
  • Democrats Schedule Votes on Abortion Access, but Lack a Legislative Path
  • California Expects Surge in Out-of-State Abortion Patients
  • Texans Warned to Conserve Electricity Amid Fear of Rolling Blackouts
  • The Right to Travel in a Post-Roe World
  • F.D.A. to Weigh Over-the-Counter Sale of Birth Control Pills
  • China’s Economy Stumbles in the Fog of Covid War
  • In a Time of Conflict, Ukraine Entrepreneurs Make War Their Business
  • Germany on Edge as Nord Stream 1 Pipeline Goes Offline for Repair
  • Bill Ackman’s SPAC to Return $4 Billion to Investors
  • A Few Words From a Master Journalist
  • A Global Deal to Tax Large Corporations Is Delayed a Year
  • Francis X. Clines, Lyrical Writer for The Times, Dies at 84
  • Twitter’s Stock Falls Further as Doubts Swirl Over Musk’s Takeover
  • Oil Companies Oppose Windfall Tax in Britain Despie Record Profits
  • Does Public Art Have an Afterlife?
  • Could Twitter Force Musk to Close the Deal? Here’s What We Know
  • What’s Next in the Elon Musk-Twitter Saga? A Court Battle
  • What Are Spam Bots and Why They’re an Issue in Elon Musk’s Twitter Deal
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Financial Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wall Street recession fears stoked by patchy US economic data
  • The case for T-bills and patience
  • Dollar surges and stocks tumble as traders weigh global growth outlook
  • First image from James Webb telescope shows most distant galaxies
  • Lipsticks, lattes . . . and now labradors: JAB’s bet on pets
  • Female entrepreneurs may cope better in harsh investing climate
  • Live news updates: Construction commodities decline on fears of China shutdowns
  • Petronas subsidiaries seized as lawyers press $15bn claim on Malaysia
  • Twitter calls Musk’s bid to scuttle $44bn deal ‘invalid and wrongful’
  • Twitter: Musk tries to hit edit button on deal
  • Executives seek briefings on Taiwan war risk
  • America’s new agricultural frontier: vertical farms
  • Can the Conservatives change to win again?
  • Bill Ackman winds down $4bn Spac after failing to find a target
  • Hedge funds cut back trading after LME nickel chaos
  • Wall Street billionaire’s son pledges to spend big in Wisconsin Senate race
  • US warns Iran planning to provide drones to Russia for war in Ukraine
  • Johnson departure will not affect arms to Ukraine, says UK Nato ambassador
  • Macron shakes up French state media with funding overhaul plans
  • Gap chief Sonia Syngal exits as company struggles to win back US shoppers
  • Global population growth hits lowest rate since 1950
  • Wefox gains $4.5bn valuation in face of insurtech sell-off
  • Tunisia’s scarred economy dealt further blow by war in Ukraine
  • US antitrust authorities probe PGA Tour over response to Saudi rival LIV
  • Business leaders warn Tory candidates over focus on corporation tax cuts
  • Europe’s economic outlook to worsen amid gas supply disruptions
  • China’s contrasting path offers the potential for uncorrelated returns
  • FirstFT: Sunak to stand firm on taxes
  • Why Parisians are wearing crop tops this summer
  • China-South Korea ETF cross-listing scheme stalls
  • Valuations are turning realistic but risks remain
  • What we know about the Abe murder
  • Abortion law: Roe vs Wade and the US constitution
  • Schools in England warn of teaching staff cuts as energy costs bite
  • Covid-19 and the rental bust-up that never came
  • UK retail sales fall as inflation hits consumers
  • Audit watchdog to overhaul UK corporate governance code
  • Sunak to stand firm on taxes until he has ‘gripped inflation’
  • The Supreme Court has weakened legal predictability in America
  • The changing face of gun owners in the US
  • Uefa aims to double value of US Champions League media rights
  • The climate crisis is no excuse for backsliding on democracy
  • Did central bank balance sheets really need to get so big?
  • Johnson, Trump and how to get rid of a strongman leader
  • FBI director warns China espionage is greatest threat to US and allies
  • Elon Musk’s new moonshot: toppling corporate law
  • Musk stirs up a legal Twitterstorm
  • These apps have eyes on your work emails
  • Ericsson, Qualcomm and Thales to trial 5G from space
  • Russia turns off gas pipeline to Germany for repairs
  • China tech stocks: M&A goes on the chopping block
  • STMicro and GlobalFoundries to build chip factory in EU tech independence push
  • I’ve been defrauded - why won’t my bank refund me?
  • Russian court allows Kazakh oil exports via key pipeline to continue
  • China’s Ganfeng Lithium buys lithium mines in Argentina
  • Emerging markets: inflation, shortages and interest rates cripple economies
  • Klarna’s valuation crashes to under $7bn in tough funding round
  • Frontline and Euronav tweak $4bn merger terms in face of opposition
  • European ESG funds buy up defence and energy stocks
  • SEC to rescind Trump-era rule for proxy advisers
  • Chinese stocks drop on threat of renewed Covid lockdowns
  • Crypto: confirmed casino
  • While politics fragments, Europe is falling into line on trade
  • European travel/Dufry: investors should pack lightly in this sector
  • Private equity: capital call financing speeds up deals
Financial Times

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-12 07:04AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong man, 26, falls to death from flat after ‘leaning out of window to retrieve wireless earphone’
  • Hong Kong veteran activist Koo Sze-yiu sentenced to 9 months in jail for planning to display coffin bearing slogans outside Beijing’s liaison office
  • Proposed Covid-19 health code for Hong Kong will not be used for any political agenda, ‘only pandemic purposes’: John Lee
  • What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new health code system to identify coronavirus cases
  • Hong Kong’s nano flats: does falling demand point to the end of container-size abodes?
  • Hong Kong’s use of electronic tracking wristbands not ‘foolproof’ solution to deter Covid-19 patients from breaching quarantine, experts say
  • Hong Kong should offer free breast cancer screening as a life-saving priority
  • Beijing lambasts top US diplomat over ‘let Hong Kong be Hong Kong’ speech, accusing him of ‘grandstanding and hypocrisy’
  • Hong Kong considers tougher penalties for residents caught feeding wild pigeons
  • Coronavirus: Care homes in Hong Kong struggle to find staff as they brace for possible sixth wave of infections
  • Macau’s citywide coronavirus lockdown confuses and frustrates residents
  • Hong Kong sweats on hottest day of year so far as temperature hits 37.4 degrees Celsius in some parts of city
  • Hong Kong’s John Lee to meet lawmakers for informal exchange on Wednesday, as some legislators raise concerns over transparency
  • Hong Kong residents pay tribute to ‘great, tough’ Shinzo Abe at Japanese consulate
  • Hong Kong police arrest driver suspected of hitting cat with car and fleeing
  • China’s banking-crisis victims promised prompt repayment, but only if they have less than US$7,450
  • ‘Look with 2 eyes’: Beijing envoy urges South Korean media to report on ‘real China’
  • Coronavirus: new variants raise questions about sustainability of China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China debt: local governments target amateur investors in new round of risky credit expansion
  • Largest Chinese patrol ship in Taiwan Strait opens up diplomatic route to law enforcement: analysts
  • US and Australia must boost Indo-Pacific security cooperation as China looms: deputy prime minister
  • Sri Lanka crisis: political turmoil may have ‘big impact’ on China ties, observers say
  • Businesses confronting new US ban on Xinjiang products say it leaves them confused
  • India to surpass China as most-populous nation in 2023
  • Shinzo Abe: Abenomics not seen as the answer for China’s economic woes with reform favoured over stimulus
  • As BA.5 fuels another surge in cases, new Omicron subvariant Centaurus is also on the march
  • Chinese scientists develop gravity detector with potential use in submarine navigation
  • Shinzo Abe funeral: Taiwanese vice-president in Tokyo but no word from Beijing on its envoy
  • China’s cash crisis culprit named by police, said to have run powerful criminal gang since 2011
  • TikTok owner ByteDance to launch new social media app for young users that will rival Instagram-like service Xiaohongshu
  • China’s foreign minister Wang Yi warns of ‘ferocious storms’ if one-China policy is abandoned by US and Taipei
  • China and Pakistan launch naval drills aimed at countering US Indo-Pacific strategy
  • Chinese smartphone maker Oppo loses to Nokia in patent infringement case in Germany, potentially affecting sales
  • Former Chinese police boss Fu Zhenghua to face court on corruption charges
  • Tencent, Alibaba and Didi fined by China antitrust watchdog for unreported merger deals as early as 2011
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe with funeral procession through heart of Tokyo
  • How Shinzo Abe’s push for reform at home and abroad made Japan stronger and safer
  • Airport staff block Sri Lanka’s president from fleeing crisis-hit country
  • Miss Universe Bhutan blazes LGBT trail as only openly gay celebrity in a land where same-sex activity was long illegal
  • Australia urges US to expand Asia military presence or face ‘catastrophic’ balance of power failure
  • Asia needs leaders of the calibre of Singapore’s Lee Kuan Yew to navigate a messy world
  • US launches Asia charm offensive to counter China’s diplomacy after Blinken-Wang Yi meet
  • South Korea’s Yonsei students under fire for suing underpaid cleaners over ‘noisy’ protests
  • Japan’s energy crisis, Covid-19 woes likely to eclipse LDP’s push to amend constitution
  • Controversial church says mother of suspect who shot Shinzo Abe is a member
  • Shinzo Abe to have private funeral, with separate ceremony for friends of Japan that may include Trump
  • China’s Ganfeng to pay nearly US$1 billion for its third lithium mine in Argentina to bolster resource for electric vehicle batteries
  • Elon Musk says it’s time for Donald Trump to ‘sail into the sunset’
  • Iran providing Russia ‘weapons-capable’ drones for war in Ukraine, US says
  • Joe Biden seeks to persuade Saudi Arabia to pump more oil as prices soar
  • Italy’s watchdog alleges TikTok breached EU privacy rules, warning the short video platform about targeted ads
  • Non-white patients get less ICU oxygen because of flawed device: study
  • First image from Nasa’s James Webb Space Telescope offers deepest-ever look at cosmos
  • Olympic star Mo Farah reveals he was taken to UK illegally using another child’s name
  • James Bond theme composer Monty Norman dies aged 94
  • New UK prime minister to replace Boris Johnson will be named on September 5
  • Vladimir Putin expands fast track Russian citizenship to all Ukraine
  • McDonald’s Russian successor hit by sanction shortages amid Ukraine war, meaning ‘no fries with that’
  • Nutty idea? Contraceptives for squirrels in UK being mulled by scientists
  • Trump says ‘life begins at 80’ but not for Biden, amid fears President’s age is affecting his leadership
  • Travel chaos: UK’s busiest airport warns of further cancellations following 40 years of passengers in 4 months
  • Hong Kong’s listed firms set for more climate risk reporting, with roll out of new consolidated ISSB standards on the horizon
  • Why Hong Kong’s community malls are crucial to the future of the city’s retail sector
  • Chinese hotpot chain Haidilao mulls spinning off overseas restaurants, separate listing in Hong Kong
  • Alibaba, BYD drag Hong Kong stocks to three-week low as Covid-19 cases, tech fines crimp optimism
  • Twitter hits back at Musk, demands US$44 billion deal be completed as it prepares for lawsuit
  • Russian gas cuts to Europe are pushing Germany towards Asian coal, but the demand won’t last
  • Morningstar finds ‘varying levels of commitment’ on setting net zero emission targets among world’s top fund managers
  • China’s new ESG disclosure guidelines need a forward-looking slant for international alignment, experts say
  • Can Hong Kong become an innovation hub for China if closed borders remain the norm? Industry executives see challenges
  • Kyoto wants Japan travel to open quicker and foreign tourists to return, though not everyone yearns to see the masses again
  • Change the food court, change the world: eco-innovator Matt Reid on sustainability and his new dining concept in Hong Kong
  • When an American illegally entered China by boat – he did it ‘out of curiosity’
  • Hong Kong’s water supplies built by the British allowed the city to grow and thrive more than any other factor
  • Want to lose weight, but always hungry? Research suggests a way to help obese people feel full while eating less
  • Netflix K-drama Extraordinary Attorney Woo: heartwarming legal drama starring Park Eun-bin really is extraordinary
  • Art Basel, Documenta and Venice Biennale gatecrashed by maverick art activist who critiques exclusivity of the art world
  • Pastel artist Nicolas Party’s vivid landscapes a reflection on our changing relationship with the natural world
  • What is a nap dress? Floral smocked dresses are popular post-Covid as women seek comfort and joy in what they wear
  • 10 richest God of Gamblers cast members – net worths, ranked: Hong Kong icons Andy Lau, Stephen Chow, Donnie Yen, Tony Leung and Chow Yun-fat make millions – but who’s the mystery billionaire on top?
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Elie Saab’s gender-bending autumn/winter 2022-23 show featured men sweeping down the runway in red carpet-ready gowns and capes
  • Big fat Ambani wedding: Mukesh hosted a glam garden extravaganza for niece Isheta Salgaocar at his US$73 million Stoke Park estate, with family including Anant, Isha and Shloka Mehta in attendance
  • Who is Cameron Norrie, Britain’s handsome No 1 tennis star? The leftie who lost to Novak Djokovic was born in South Africa, has an American girlfriend and has earned over US$6.3 million in prize money
  • What do all 3 of Elon Musk’s baby mamas have in common? Meet Shivon Zilis, the Neuralink tech whizz who shares twins with the Tesla CEO, recent girlfriend Grimes and author Justine Wilson
  • 6 female child stars who quit Hollywood – and what they did next: from Mary-Kate and Ashley Olsen to The Amanda Show’s Amanda Bynes and iCarly’s Jennette McCurdy
  • Meet Marc Kalman, Bella Hadid’s elusive art director boyfriend – the supermodel’s new partner has been described as ‘a young Fabien Baron, Charles Saatchi and Steve Jobs rolled into one’
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 12 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-12 07:01AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: NASA’s James Webb telescope images revealed; NSW COVID reinfection period reduced from 12 weeks to four
  • Business lobby demands deadline on new COVID advice after ‘blow to CBD’
  • Brisbane woman pleads guilty to attempted murder after father begged her to end his life
  • Andrew Mehrtens puts rugby governing body on blast
  • Albanese blames Greens for a decade of inaction on climate policy
  • Why the Victorian government won’t go harder on COVID restrictions
  • Leaders gather for Clean Energy Forum in Sydney
  • Everything you need to know before Origin III
  • Man shot following police pursuit in Sydney
  • Bullseye: Dart-focused food and beverage venues score big city sites
  • Big business backs targeted sick pay during virus surge
  • Chinese bank scam victims to be repaid after protests turn violent
  • No more free RATs despite push to retest sooner
  • Hazlewood policy weakens case for Warner and Head to play in India
  • Preserved coffee beans, more than 167 years old found by archaeologists working on Metro Tunnel
  • Victorian government rejected CHO health advice, instead settles for ‘strongly recommending’ masks
  • Laundy family grabs Port Maquarie pub as Dixon sells Nag’s Head
  • Jason Roberts to sue Victorian government and police
  • Australia’s Sri Lanka tour more profound than wins and losses
  • Why the Wests Tigers think they’ve salvaged the Isaiah Papali’i deal
  • Household spending continues at pace despite dropping confidence
  • Prosecution of Bernard Collaery was an assault on values Australia holds dear
  • Best of cartoons, July 12, 2022
  • Australia campaigns to co-host target-setting UN climate summit with the Pacific
  • Masks should be worn indoors: Health Minister
  • Energy, building giant to increase renewables investment in $26 billion plan
  • Torn up EBAs behind low wage growth: Treasurer
  • Free trade deal with Europe can be served up in 12 months: trade minister
  • Albanese aims for superpower status in global energy transition
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 11, 2022
  • Could climate change help thaw relations between Beijing and Canberra?
  • Antivirals under the radar as drug company rolls out own COVID health campaign
  • Skills crisis the ‘number one challenge’ for employers at jobs summit
  • Australia prepares for likelihood of climate refugees, backs push for UN justice
  • Former Gladys Berejiklian staffer convicted of historical child sex abuse
  • Man shot after being stopped by police in Sydney’s inner west
  • NSW COVID-19 reinfection period reduced from 12 weeks to four
  • Randwick Barracks accommodation proposal angers residents
  • Man charged with manslaughter over forklift death in Sydney’s west
  • ‘Total disbelief’: Unnecessary medical test killed Carmel Haynes
  • Catholic women want to be at the decision table
  • In the Herald: July 12, 1955
  • Cutting through the invisible barriers
  • Private and independent schools awarded vast majority of $30,000 Ramsay Centre scholarships
  • ICAC considers probe into John Barilaro’s US trade post
  • ‘Terrible tragedy’: Inquest examines stabbing death of Sydney teacher
  • Penny Wong's pitch for Pacific unity
  • Can’t keep a bad man down: What next for Boris Johnson?
  • First James Webb telescope image looks further into space – and time – than ever
  • Is this road in Siberia or Japan? Expert Google Maps players can tell you in 0.1 seconds
  • ‘This isn’t House of Cards’: Johnson to remain PM until September amid ‘poisonous’ contest
  • New York releases nuclear war safety video
  • Putin offers fast-track citizenship to all Ukrainians
  • NASA’s James Webb Telescope reveals first colour image
  • Faroe Islands sets quota of 500 dolphins to be killed in controversial annual whale hunt
  • London bakes in summer heat wave
  • Mo Farah reveals his real identity
  • Marles warns Australia, US must step up to avoid ‘catastrophic failure’ in Indo-Pacific
  • ‘New Zealand got it wrong’: The man vying to topple Jacinda Ardern and open up the nation
  • Abe’s legacy? Japan a step closer to reawakening warrior spirit
  • Playing piano and swimming, Sri Lankan protesters settle into presidential palace
  • Man charged with murder over train station stabbing remanded in custody
  • From the Archives, 1997: Canberra hospital demolition ends in tragedy
  • Fremantle Dockers speak out after players cop racist abuse online
  • Qld hospitals brace for new COVID peak as health chief stands by mask rules
  • Monty Norman: composer who wrote celebrated James Bond theme tune
  • Perth to Singapore flight forced to make emergency landing after tyres overheat
  • Stealth bombers fly into Brisbane in US show of force to region
  • Woman falls three stories from unit block in Sydney's west
  • Annie Cannon-Brookes snaps up Dunk Island for $24 million
  • The buy now, pay later bubble is bursting before our very eyes
  • ASX gains despite Wall Street declines, which sent big tech tumbling
  • BNPL bloodbath: Zip merger falls apart as CBA takes $2b hit on Klarna
  • China’s response to bank protests hints at a wider problem
  • Ban the bench: Scientists bid to stop cancer-causing building material
  • Australia’s biggest super fund braces for prolonged slowdown
  • ‘Nightmare scenario’: Looming legal battle with Musk hammers Twitter’s shares
  • World’s biggest money manager warns against hunting for share bargains
  • Former Formula One supremo Bernie Ecclestone charged with UK tax fraud
  • Tech giants back Australia’s first solar-powered carbon capture project
  • No ‘presenteeism’: Small businesses pause office return as winter COVID-19 wave worsens
  • NAB to defend trader’s unequal pay complaint
  • ‘Quality issues’: Bad oranges bruise Costa Group shares
  • Paul Walter Hauser’s serial killer flies away with the honours in Black Bird
  • Cinema’s big mystery: Who killed the man who made the first ever film?
  • ‘An own goal’: Experts criticise police efforts to ‘censor’ Australian rappers
  • The old lady in Titanic is my spiteful, minimalist queen
  • YouTube removes internet’s favourite background music over bogus copyright claim
  • Australian Chamber Orchestra brings beloved children’s book to life
  • At a Yarra Valley cellar door, this Figaro is in hipster moustache heaven
  • The best toy in a Kinder Surprise? A world of language
  • Alex the Astronaut: ‘I was already in therapy for all that other stuff, why not add one more thing?’
  • Ten’s Pilot Showcase lets viewers taste-test six new shows. Were they any good?
  • The ‘scary’ new role bringing Michael Sheen to Australia
  • Julia Gillard to take ground-breaking misogyny speech to the stage
  • Wedding Singer musical romps home with a swag of Green Room gongs
  • Torn in half by war, Vasily Petrenko sees hope for peace in music
  • Forty years on, Blade Runner’s still peerless as a sci-fi classic
  • First pictures of Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan reunion on Neighbours revealed
  • Atlassian to donate Russian revenue as Ukrainian protestors demand full boycott
  • Song of the elusive pygmy blue whale reveals numbers on the rebound
  • National weather forecast for Tuesday July 12
  • Greens’ pitch to ditch gas signals energy as an election issue
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
